commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram dispatch and shared reply-runtime helpers so Telegram suppresses transient previews when visible source replies resolve to message-tool-only delivery.

PR surface: Source +115, Tests +236. Total +351 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level behavior: current main creates Telegram draft/reasoning streams before the later source-reply policy can suppress automatic source delivery. Live current-main reproduction was not rerun in this read-only review, but the linked issue and PR discussion include real Telegram observations.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is maintainer review of the SDK boundary plus contributor-visible Telegram proof quality, not an automated code repair.

target.title: [Repo Assist] fix(setup): add Windows-side gateway health check to pair-operator step
target.body:
🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Closes #662

## Root Cause

`PairOperatorStep.ExecuteAsync()` was missing the Windows-side HTTP gateway health check that `PairNodeStep` already has (line 2086). Both steps attempt WebSocket connections to `(localhost/redacted) but only `pair-node` first verified the gateway was reachable from the Windows host.

After `StartGatewayStep` confirms the gateway is up (by running `curl` **inside WSL** to `127.0.0.1:{port}`), WSL port forwarding may take a few seconds to propagate to the Windows loopback interface. `PairOperatorStep` immediately tried the WebSocket connect with no probe, resulting in:

- v0.6.0: `SocketException 10061 (Connection refused)` — port not yet forwarded
- v0.6.3: HTTP 5-second timeout — same forwarding lag, just surfaced differently

## Fix

Added the same Windows-side probe that `PairNodeStep` uses, immediately before the WebSocket connect:

```csharp
// Verify gateway is reachable from Windows before connecting (WSL port forwarding may lag)
try
{
    using var http = new HttpClient { Timeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5) };
    var resp = await http.GetAsync($"(localhost/redacted) ct);
    ctx.Logger.Debug($"Gateway health check: HTTP {(int)resp.StatusCode}");
}
catch (Exception ex)
{
    return StepResult.Fail($"Gateway not reachable before operator pairing: {ex.Message}");
}
```

The step already has `RetryPolicy Retry => new(MaxAttempts: 3, InitialDelay: TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3))`, so if the health check fails on the first attempt the step will retry up to 3 times with increasing delays, giving WSL port forwarding time to propagate before the WebSocket connect.

## Trade-offs

- The HTTP probe adds one extra network round-trip (~5s max) to the happy path when the gateway is already reachable. Given this is a setup step, the extra latency is acceptable.
- The probe is a GET to `(localhost/redacted) so it doesn't require a token. Any HTTP response (200, 401, 403) is treated as "reachable" — matching `PairNodeStep`'s pattern.
- No new dependencies or interfaces added.

## Test Status

Added a new regression test `PairOperatorStep_FailsWhenGatewayNotReachableFromWindows` that allocates a free TCP port, releases it, and verifies the step returns `StepOutcome.Failed` with a "not reachable" message.

```
SetupEngine tests: Failed: 0, Passed: 248 (1 new test), Skipped: 0
```

Pre-existing failures on Linux CI:
- `OpenClaw.SetupEngine.Tests`: 1 pre-existing failure (`Constructor_SetsLocalDataDirFromLocalAppDataRootEnvironment` — Windows path separator in Linux test environment)
- `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests`: 8 pre-existing failures (Windows sandbox/networking tests)
- `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests`: ✅ 1038 passed, 0 failed
